description: Hand-built evaluation set for TenancyMate over the Residential Tenancies Act 1986. Every expected section was verified against the parsed corpus; answer points state what a correct answer must convey. Questions with answerable=false are outside the Act and the assistant must say so.

pairs.question: How many weeks of bond can my landlord ask for?
answerPoints: a general bond may not exceed 4 weeks' rent

pairs.question: Can a landlord ask for a guarantee or extra security on top of the bond?
answerPoints: no security other than a permitted bond may be required

pairs.question: Is it legal for a landlord to charge a fee just for granting the tenancy?
answerPoints: requiring key money is prohibited

pairs.question: What must the landlord do with my bond after I pay it?
answerPoints: forward the bond to the chief executive within 23 working days
give a receipt

pairs.question: How do I get my bond back when the tenancy ends?
answerPoints: application to the chief executive for payment of the bond
agreed applications or applications without agreement

pairs.question: Can I pay my bond directly to Tenancy Services instead of the landlord?
answerPoints: tenant may pay the bond direct to the chief executive with the landlord's consent

pairs.question: How much rent can be demanded in advance?
answerPoints: no more than 2 weeks' rent in advance
cannot require further rent before it falls due

pairs.question: How often can my rent be increased and how much notice is needed?
answerPoints: at least 60 days' written notice
no increase within 12 months of the start or the last increase

pairs.question: My rent seems way above similar places. Can I challenge it?
answerPoints: Tribunal may determine the market rent
rent substantially exceeding market rent

pairs.question: Does my landlord have to give me receipts for rent paid in cash?
answerPoints: receipts required for cash rent payments

pairs.question: What records does a landlord have to keep about the tenancy?
answerPoints: landlord must keep rent and tenancy records

pairs.question: Can I recover rent I paid above a Tribunal-set market rent?
answerPoints: rent in excess of market rent order is irrecoverable by the landlord and recoverable by the tenant

pairs.question: Can rent go up mid-term if the landlord substantially improves the place at my request?
answerPoints: rent may increase by agreement or order after substantial improvements

pairs.question: My landlord keeps showing up unannounced and it feels like harassment. What are my rights?
answerPoints: tenant is entitled to quiet enjoyment
interference amounts to harassment

pairs.question: How much notice does a landlord need before entering to inspect the property?
answerPoints: at least 48 hours' notice for inspection
between 8 am and 7 pm
24 hours for repairs

pairs.question: Can I change the locks without asking my landlord?
answerPoints: locks may not be altered without the other party's consent

pairs.question: Whose job is it to fix the leaking roof and keep the house in reasonable repair?
answerPoints: landlord must provide and maintain the premises in a reasonable state of repair
comply with health and safety requirements

pairs.question: What am I responsible for as a tenant day to day?
answerPoints: keep premises reasonably clean and tidy
notify the landlord of damage
not use premises for unlawful purposes

pairs.question: Am I liable if my visitor damages the property?
answerPoints: tenant responsible for actions of people at the premises with consent

pairs.question: Who pays the council rates and who pays the power bill?
answerPoints: landlord pays rates and insurance type outgoings
tenant pays for services consumed like electricity

pairs.question: Can I take the shelves I installed with me when I move out?
answerPoints: tenant fixtures may be removed
premises must be restored

pairs.question: Does a tenancy agreement have to be in writing?
answerPoints: agreement must be in writing and signed

pairs.question: What details must a tenancy agreement include?
answerPoints: names and addresses of the parties
rent, bond and address of the premises

pairs.question: My landlord moved to Australia for six months. Do they need an agent in NZ?
answerPoints: agent required if out of New Zealand longer than 21 consecutive days

pairs.question: A landlord rejected me because I have kids. Is that allowed?
answerPoints: discrimination in tenancy matters is an unlawful act

pairs.question: Can a 17 year old legally sign a tenancy agreement?
answerPoints: tenancy agreements with minors are enforceable as if of full age

pairs.question: Can I sublet my room without the landlord's permission?
answerPoints: subletting requires the landlord's written consent
consent may not be unreasonably withheld where the agreement allows

pairs.question: Does the landlord have to tell me before putting the house up for sale?
answerPoints: landlord must give notice if premises are put on the market

pairs.question: I signed a lease but the old tenant is still living there on day one. What now?
answerPoints: landlord must provide vacant possession at the start of the tenancy

pairs.question: I found out my rental is an illegal converted garage. Where do I stand?
answerPoints: landlord must ensure there is no legal impediment to occupation

pairs.question: My landlord took my TV because I owe rent. Can they do that?
answerPoints: tenant's goods must not be seized

pairs.question: My agreement says all remaining rent becomes due if I breach it. Is that enforceable?
answerPoints: accelerated rent or damages provisions are prohibited

pairs.question: How much notice must a landlord give to end a periodic tenancy without a reason?
answerPoints: landlord must give at least 90 days' notice
42 days applies only on specific grounds
tenant gives 21 days

pairs.question: I complained about mould and got a termination notice a week later. Can I fight it?
answerPoints: Tribunal may declare a notice retaliatory

pairs.question: How far behind on rent do I have to be before the landlord can apply to end the tenancy?
answerPoints: 21 days of rent arrears
Tribunal may terminate

pairs.question: The house was badly damaged in a storm and we cannot live there. Can the tenancy end?
answerPoints: destruction or premises rendered uninhabitable allow termination

pairs.question: What happens when my one year fixed-term lease runs out and nobody says anything?
answerPoints: fixed-term becomes periodic unless notice of contrary intention is given

pairs.question: By when must I exercise a right of renewal in my fixed-term lease?
answerPoints: right to renew or extend must be exercised at least 21 days before expiry

pairs.question: My flatmate vanished owing rent and left the flat empty. What can the landlord do?
answerPoints: landlord may take possession where premises are abandoned
Tribunal order

pairs.question: The previous tenant left furniture behind. What is the landlord supposed to do with it?
answerPoints: procedures for goods left on premises
assessment of market value before disposal

pairs.question: Someone is squatting in my rental property. How do I get them out legally?
answerPoints: eviction of squatters through the Tribunal
entry without an order is prohibited

pairs.question: My income collapsed and I cannot afford my fixed-term lease. Can it be reduced or ended early?
answerPoints: Tribunal may reduce or terminate a fixed-term tenancy for unforeseen hardship

pairs.question: How much bond can a boarding house operator charge?
answerPoints: boarding house bond of 1 week's rent or less

pairs.question: What happens to the tenancy if the only tenant dies?
answerPoints: tenancy may be terminated following the death of a sole tenant

pairs.question: How do I declare my rental income for income tax?
answerPoints: the Act does not address income tax

pairs.question: Does my landlord have to check my visa status before renting to me?
answerPoints: the Act does not impose immigration checks
